channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Capcom Spotlight Set for March 5
item.content:encoded:
A Capcom Spotlight presentation is set for this Thursday, March 5. The company announced the upcoming presentation on social media.

Among the games featured will be Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, PRAGMATA, Street Fighter 6, Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection, and Mega Man Dual Override.

Dual Override is one of the only games announced that doesn’t have a release date. It was first shown off at the 2025 Game Awards. Monster Hunter will be released next Friday, March 13. Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection will be released 2 weeks later, on March 27.

PRAGMATA is slated for release on April 24, and Street Fighter 6 has been on the market for quite some time.

The event will be broadcast at 2:00 p.m. PST and 5:00 p.m. on the East Coast. The presentation will be about 35 minutes in length. It’s also noted that it will be broadcast in 13 languages and that the date and time can change without notice. The presentation will also feature certain age-restricted titles.

Capcom is currently firing on all cylinders following the release of Resident Evil Requiem. The game is one of the best-reviewed titles of 2025 and an early contender for game of the year. Check out how to solve that game’s final puzzle here.

What are you hoping to see at the next Capcom Spotlight event? What kind of surprise games, if any, do you think there will be?

MOBILE APP

item.title: GTA Vice City Stories Sales Data Revealed After Trademark Dispute
item.content:encoded:
Sales data for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City Stories has been revealed as Rockstar Games’ parent company, Take-Two Interactive, took action to stop a sandwich shop from naming itself Vice City Subs.

On February 13, 2026, Take-Two Interactive filed an opposition to a trademark for Vice City Subs with the USPTO. The latter would have become a sandwich shop. In the opposition letter, Take-Two tout the success of the Grand Theft Auto brand as a whole, noting that GTA 5 grossed over 1 billion dollars in retail sales in just 3 days after its release back in September 2013.

GTA Vice City Stories Sold Over 7 Million Copies Globally

The Vice City games are also widely regarded as immensely popular. The PlayStation 2 release of Grand Theft Auto: Vice City is noted as having sold dozens of millions of copies worldwide, 17.5 million by 2008. However, the news concerns an updated sales figure for Grand Theft Auto: Vice City Stories, a PlayStation Portable spin-off of the aforementioned PS2 game.

The filing notes that Vice City Stories, first released in 2006, has sold over 7 million copies worldwide. Back in 2008, Take-Two had listed the game as selling 4.5 million copies. This means that the game has sold at least 2.5 million copies in the last 18 years.

The Future of GTA Heads Back to Vice City

As mentioned above, Grand Theft Auto 5 was released nearly 13 years ago in September 2013. Thankfully, the next mainline GTA game is on the way. Grand Theft Auto 6 is expected to be released in November 2026. The game has been delayed numerous times, was once planned for 2025, and, before the most recent delay, was scheduled for May 2026.

This next game will take place in Vice City and is expected to be one of the biggest game releases of its time. Learn about the latest locations planned for the upcoming game at this link.

How do you feel about this action from Take-Two to stop a sandwich shop from using the Vice City name? How excited are you to go back to Vice City in Grand Theft Auto 6? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fallout Remasters Developer May Have Just Revealed Itself in a Strange Way
item.content:encoded:
The Fallout 3 and Fallout: New Vegas remasters are still on everyone’s minds following recent revelations. It was claimed mere weeks ago that both projects are underway and progressing nicely, and even Todd Howard has admitted that ‘multiple Fallout projects’ are in the works.

In a recent post on a social media platform you wouldn’t expect, the developer behind the Fallout remasters may have revealed itself, and piecing the clues together, it makes total sense.

Is Iron Galaxy Developing the Fallout Remasters?

In a post on LinkedIn, of all places, Iron Galaxy Studios made a clear and direct nod to the Fallout franchise in a way that has fans excited. The team posted an image of a 72-slide presentation deck that was titled ‘Company Meeting – February 2026.’

The first slide of that deck? An image of the iconic ‘Please Stand By’ title card that has become synonymous with the Fallout brand.

Looking closer at the scenery, it even appeared to be pulled from Fallout: New Vegas, the 2010 open-world epic that remains one of the most beloved games ever made.

Fans have busied themselves trying to scour every scrap of the image for any more information, even going so far as to read into the ’70 F’ temperature reading on the thermostat to the side of the picture.

It adds up, though.

Iron Galaxy Studios has forged a critical connection with Bethesda Game Studios over the years. The team has contributed to the development of Fallout 76 and The Elder Scrolls Online, and it assisted years ago with both Skyrim and Fallout 4’s VR variants.

Oh, and the company also helped bring Skyrim to life on Nintendo Switch as a stellar port. This proves the studio has legs in the remaster game.

It could be nothing but a red herring, but it could also be a direct indication that Iron Galaxy Studios is the place to be for anyone wanting to know more about the Fallout remasters.

We’ve reached out to Iron Galaxy to probe for more information, but that’s a pipe dream at this point.

Do you think Iron Galaxy is working on the Fallout remaster projects? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Gray Zone Warfare is getting hideout and crafting mechanics

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Gray Zone Warfare Hideout and Crafting Planned for Major 0.5 Update
item.content:encoded:
Gray Zone Warfare, the open-world tactical shooter, could be getting a long-awaited feature soon. The game’s next major update beyond 0.4, which was revealed Friday by MADFINGER Games, should accommodate deeper hideout mechanics and in-game crafting – and that’s 0.5 that we’re talking about.

This builds on the faction base feature and the combat outpost elements added in recent months, giving players more of a home inside the world of Lamang. It also allows for further customization and ownership, empowering players to not just buy and find gear, but to craft it with their own hands (digital hands) and resources.

Gray Zone Warfare Players Are Getting a Home Away from Home

Plenty of extraction shooters out there use hideout mechanics, and even though Gray Zone Warfare isn’t officially an extraction shooter anymore, that doesn’t mean it can’t adopt the concept.

MADFINGER Games just unveiled everything that’s coming with the 0.4 update in Gray Zone. It’s a substantial patch that changes many of the game’s core elements, adds 100 new tasks, new movement mechanics, biomes, and more. In the words of the team, it effectively makes it feel like a new game.

But there’s much more planned, of course.

In the 0.5 update, which doesn’t have a date at present, players are expected to receive a hideout mechanic and crafting. The details are thin, but based on what we can assume compared to other, similar games, this will give players a customizable place to call home in Lamang.

It’s unclear if the hideout will be incorporated within the faction bases that have existed since the game launched in April 2024, or if they’ll be more dynamic and can be placed around the game world.

In ARC Raiders, an extraction adventure title, the player’s hideout is located in Speranza, a safe zone away from the trials and tribulations of ‘topside’, and it’s the place from which they launch missions into the game’s maps. That’s also how it works in Escape from Tarkov and Incursion: Red River, for example.

But Gray Zone Warfare has proven more than once that it’s willing to break molds and be innovative when it comes to core concepts, so the notion of a hideout mechanic could be blown out of the water by MADFINGER Games.

Are you excited to see hideouts land in Gray Zone Warfare? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Netflix has backed out of the Warner Bros purchase

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
